name: Larry Bird
distinctive_detail: Took the same franchise he carried as a player and rebuilt it from the front office and bench, winning Coach of the Year, Executive of the Year, and MVP across three different jobs.
work_style: Ran everything through his own brutal standard and trusted his read over consensus, on the floor and later in the chair.
career_traits: stayed inside one organization in three capacities
set the standard others measured against
2k_label: The Architect Who Could Also Play
comp_tone: Earned, dry, total respect. For the person who built the thing and then ran the thing.

name: Magic Johnson
distinctive_detail: Built a second identity entirely outside the game as an operator and investor after his career was cut short by a forced exit, becoming bigger off the floor than most are on it.
work_style: Made everyone around him better by design, then took that same connective instinct into building businesses from scratch.
career_traits: turned a forced ending into a launch
2k_label: The One Who Got Bigger After
comp_tone: Generous, expansive, builder energy. For the connector whose second act is the real story.

name: Isiah Thomas
distinctive_detail: Brilliant on-floor leader and franchise builder whose post-playing executive runs repeatedly ended in political flameouts that overshadowed real wins.
work_style: Led with force and charisma, built and ran organizations, but burned as many bridges as he crossed.
comp_tone: [QA] Honest about the political-exit shadow on his executive runs; verify the user wants the full arc and not just the playing legacy.

name: Jerry West
distinctive_detail: Became a far greater team architect than even his Hall of Fame playing career, building or shaping multiple title rosters as a GM and front-office consultant across four franchises.
work_style: Obsessive talent evaluator who could not stand losing and translated a clutch player's edge into roster construction.
career_traits: the logo who became the executive
comp_tone: Reverent, sharp. For the person whose building work outran their already-great first act.

name: Pat Riley
distinctive_detail: Went from limited role player to defining coach to franchise-building executive, standing up a championship culture in Miami from essentially nothing and keeping it alive for decades.
work_style: Imposed a non-negotiable standard and culture, reinvented his own role three times to stay load-bearing.
comp_tone: Commanding, earned. For the serial reinventor who builds the whole culture, not just the team.

name: Gregg Popovich
distinctive_detail: Hired himself as coach after running basketball operations and built one of the most durable organizations in sports, stable enough that everyone wanted to work inside it.
work_style: Built a system and a value set that outlasted any individual player and made the institution the star.
comp_tone: Dry, deep respect. For the founder who built a place people want to belong to and never leave.

name: Steve Nash
distinctive_detail: An overlooked, traded-away prospect who became a two-time MVP in his thirties by running an offense that redefined how the game was played.
work_style: Made an entire system work through vision and pace, peaking late once he finally got the keys.
comp_tone: Warm, smart. For the person who got the keys late and made everything around them run.

name: Chauncey Billups
distinctive_detail: Bounced through five teams in his first six seasons before landing in Detroit, where he became Mr. Big Shot and a Finals MVP running the offense and taking the last shot.
work_style: A high-usage closer who controlled the game's final possessions, found his home only after years of being moved.
comp_tone: Earned, clutch. For the closer who got passed around before becoming the one who takes the final shot.

name: Robert Horry
distinctive_detail: Modest regular-season role player who hit so many series-deciding shots across three franchises he earned the nickname Big Shot Rob and seven rings.
work_style: Disappeared for stretches then took and made the single biggest shot of the night when it mattered.
comp_tone: Sly, accurate. For the quiet operator who only shows up huge exactly when the stakes are highest.

name: Manu Ginobili
distinctive_detail: Drafted 57th, played years in Europe, then came over and accepted a sixth-man role for his whole prime despite being good enough to start, climbing into the Hall of Fame the unglamorous way.
work_style: Brought instant chaos-creating offense off the bench and put ego aside for the role the team needed.
career_traits: long developmental road before the NBA
chose bench role for the team
comp_tone: Beloved, dry. For the slow-burn riser who took the long way and put the team over the title.

name: John Stockton
distinctive_detail: Played all 19 seasons for one franchise, never flashy, and quietly became the all-time leader in assists and steals through pure unglamorous consistency.
work_style: Showed up, ran the offense the same exact way for two decades, and let the compounding numbers speak.
comp_tone: Quiet, exact. For the unflashy lifer whose numbers only make sense after twenty years of the same thing.

name: Tim Duncan
distinctive_detail: Played 19 seasons with one franchise as the quiet, fundamental anchor, never seeking spotlight, the steady foundation everything was built on.
work_style: Did the same reliable, fundamentally perfect things every night for nearly two decades with zero ego or drama.
comp_tone: Reserved, deep respect. For the no-drama anchor everything else gets built on top of.

name: Udonis Haslem
distinctive_detail: Undrafted, played 20 seasons for his hometown team, spending his last years barely playing but holding the locker room together as the keeper of the culture.
work_style: Did the dirty work early, then transitioned into the unofficial standard-keeper who mentored everyone who came through.
comp_tone: Warm, dignified. For the undrafted lifer who became the institution's conscience.

name: Ben Wallace
distinctive_detail: Undrafted, told he was too short to play center, became a four-time Defensive Player of the Year and the anchor of a title team without ever needing the ball.
work_style: Built an All-Star career entirely out of effort, defense, and rebounding, things that do not show in the box score.
career_traits: value invisible on the stat sheet
comp_tone: Blue-collar pride. For the person whose whole value is the work nobody puts in the highlight reel.

name: Dennis Rodman
distinctive_detail: Did not play organized ball until his twenties, then became the best rebounder of his era by mastering one elite skill while ignoring scoring entirely.
work_style: Obsessively perfected a single high-value skill and accepted being a non-scorer to be irreplaceable at one thing.
comp_tone: [QA] Use for the off-floor antics only if the user fits; the career fact is the elite single-skill specialist who started late.

name: PJ Tucker
distinctive_detail: Washed out of the NBA after one rookie year, spent five seasons grinding overseas in five countries, then came back and carved a long career as a defensive specialist who never needed plays run for him.
work_style: Defends the toughest assignment, hits the corner three, complains about nothing, and survives by doing exactly one job extremely well.
comp_tone: Gritty, respectful. For the person who got cut, went and proved it elsewhere, and came back to do one job perfectly.

name: Shane Battier
distinctive_detail: The no-stats All-Star: contributed almost entirely in ways the box score missed, doing the unselfish dirty work that made good teams great.
work_style: Took the hard defensive assignment and made the smart play, valuable precisely because his impact was invisible.
comp_tone: Cerebral. For the complementary piece who makes a built thing better, not the one who builds it.

name: Draymond Green
distinctive_detail: A second-round pick who became the defensive and emotional engine of a dynasty by doing everything except score, the brain of the operation despite never being the talent.
work_style: Quarterbacks the defense, makes the connective plays, and runs his mouth as the team's enforcer and conscience.
comp_tone: Fiery, sharp. For the connective brain who runs the whole thing without ever being the headline.

name: Andre Iguodala
distinctive_detail: Gave up being a leading scorer and All-Star to become a bench role player and Finals MVP, reinventing himself for a winning team late in his career.

comp_tone: Measured. For the person who stepped down in title to step up in value.

name: Marcus Smart
distinctive_detail: Won Defensive Player of the Year as a guard, valued for emotional fire and defense rather than scoring, the spark plug a team rallies around.

name: Jrue Holiday
distinctive_detail: A quiet two-way guard who repeatedly accepts a smaller role on better teams, doing the selfless winning work that earns universal respect from peers.

name: Patrick Beverley
distinctive_detail: Went undrafted, spent years overseas, then built a career almost entirely on irritating defense and outsized energy on a string of different teams.

career_traits: career built on edge and energy

name: Tony Allen
distinctive_detail: Built an entire respected career on lockdown perimeter defense alone, coining First Team All-Defense, First Team All-Offense not allowed about himself.

name: Bruce Bowen
distinctive_detail: Went undrafted and played years in France and the minor leagues before becoming an elite 3-and-D specialist on title teams in his late twenties.

name: Derrick White
distinctive_detail: Played Division II ball, was barely recruited, climbed through the developmental ranks, and became a quietly indispensable two-way guard and champion.

name: Al Horford
distinctive_detail: Spent a long, steady, unglamorous career as the reliable adult in the room before finally winning a title in his late thirties.

name: Danny Green

distinctive_detail: Barely played but stuck around for over a decade as a beloved end-of-bench locker-room presence who knew exactly what his job was.

distinctive_detail: Entered as a quiet defensive role player, said almost nothing, and methodically built himself into a two-way superstar and Finals MVP through relentless private work.

name: Jimmy Butler

distinctive_detail: A skilled, cerebral big man who thrived as a high-level complementary star, the smart selfless piece that turned a contender into a champion.

name: Vince Carter
distinctive_detail: Reinvented himself from a high-flying franchise scorer into a respected 22-season veteran bench shooter and mentor, the longest career in league history.

name: Brandon Roy

2k_label: The Player Who Was Also The Voice
comp_tone: [QA] Use only for a genuine parallel-second-identity person, not as a lazy reinvention default. Assign to anyone.

name: Cynthia Cooper
distinctive_detail: Played years overseas in relative obscurity and did not become a dominant star until her mid-thirties when a new league finally gave her a stage, then won four straight titles as the closer. Assignable to any user regardless of gender.
work_style: Took the big shot and carried the team, finally getting the spotlight late after a long career in the shadows abroad.
career_traits: made the most of a late chance
comp_tone: Earned. For the person who waited years for the right stage and dominated the second it arrived. Assign to anyone.

name: Lisa Leslie
distinctive_detail: Was a face-of-the-league anchor who helped legitimize a brand-new league from its founding, then built a second career as a broadcaster and executive. Assignable to any user regardless of gender.
work_style: Anchored both ends as a franchise centerpiece while serving as a public face who helped a new institution take root.
2k_label: The Face Of A New League
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the founding-era face and anchor identity; verify which second-act detail to use. Assign to anyone.

name: Sheryl Swoopes
distinctive_detail: Was the first player signed to a brand-new league and became its first three-time MVP, a foundational star who built credibility for an institution from day one. Assignable to any user regardless of gender.
work_style: Scored and defended at an elite two-way level as the cornerstone the whole new enterprise was built around.
career_traits: first player signed to the league
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the first-signing/foundational-MVP identity; verify exact firsts if quoted. Assign to anyone.

name: Sylvia Fowles
distinctive_detail: A dominant, durable defensive-anchor center who quietly trained the entire time to become a mortician, building a literal second profession outside the game. Assignable to any user regardless of gender.
work_style: Anchored the paint and rebounded relentlessly while preparing a genuine, unrelated second career off the court.
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the defensive-anchor identity and the mortician second-career detail; verify if quoting directly. Assign to anyone.

name: A'ja Wilson
distinctive_detail: A franchise cornerstone and multiple-time MVP who is the clear first option and closer, the player the entire operation runs through. Assignable to any user regardless of gender.
work_style: Carries the scoring load, anchors the defense, and takes the big shot as the undisputed centerpiece. Assign to anyone.
comp_tone: Confident. For the person the whole operation is built around and runs through. Assign to anyone, any gender.

name: Lauren Jackson
distinctive_detail: A dominant MVP-level scorer whose body and injuries cut her prime short, who later made an improbable comeback to play again years after retiring. Assignable to any user regardless of gender.
work_style: Scored at an elite level as the centerpiece, fought through injuries that shortened her peak, and returned against the odds.
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the injury-shortened prime and comeback; verify which chapter the user identifies with. Assign to anyone.

name: Maya Moore
distinctive_detail: Was a peak, championship-level superstar who walked away from the game at her prime to pursue criminal-justice reform, choosing an entirely different calling over basketball. Assignable to any user regardless of gender.
work_style: Dominated as a two-way star, then deliberately left the thing she was best at to commit fully to a cause that mattered more.
comp_tone: Reflective. For the person who left the thing they were best at for something they believed mattered more. Assign to anyone.

name: Breanna Stewart
distinctive_detail: A dominant two-way superstar who tore her Achilles at her peak, missed a full season, and rebuilt herself back to MVP level, then changed teams to win on her own terms. Assignable to any user regardless of gender.
work_style: Did everything at an elite level, fought back from a devastating injury, and took control of her own career path.
2k_label: The One Who Rebuilt The Peak
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the Achilles injury and full return to MVP form; verify the team-change framing if quoted. Assign to anyone.

name: Boris Diaw
distinctive_detail: A do-everything connective forward who was waived as out-of-shape, bounced teams, then became an indispensable passing glue piece on a champion by fitting into something already built.
work_style: Made the extra pass, set screens, defended versatile, and made a built team flow without ever being the focus.
comp_tone: Easygoing. For the connector who slots into a built thing and makes it flow, not the one who builds it.

name: Kyle Korver
distinctive_detail: A second-round pick who built a 17-year career on being one of the greatest pure shooters ever, elite at exactly one thing for nearly two decades.
work_style: Ran off screens, hit the open three at a historic rate, and survived a long career by perfecting one elite skill.
comp_tone: Clean. For the person who is genuinely one of the best ever at a single thing and built a career on it.

name: Zach Randolph
distinctive_detail: Was a problem-child high-usage scorer early whose reputation cratered, then reinvented himself as the beloved blue-collar heart of a gritty contender.
work_style: Bullied the post for buckets and boards, and turned a troubled early reputation into being the soul of a tough team.
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the troubled-early-then-beloved-veteran arc; handle the early-reputation framing with care.

name: Bam Adebayo
distinctive_detail: An elite defensive anchor and connective passing big who does the unglamorous work that holds a team together rather than chasing his own scoring numbers.
work_style: Guards all five positions, makes the connective pass, and accepts a foundational role over a flashy scoring one.
comp_tone: Steady. For the person who holds the whole thing together by doing the work that does not get the headlines.

name: Tyrese Haliburton
distinctive_detail: Slid in the draft over doubts, was traded away by the team that drafted him, then became an elite pass-first All-NBA floor general who makes everyone around him better.
work_style: Runs the offense unselfishly, prioritizes the assist over his own shot, and elevates a whole roster.
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the slid-in-draft, traded-away, pass-first arc; verify if leaning on the trade detail.

name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
distinctive_detail: Was traded as a young secondary piece, then quietly built himself season by season into an MVP-level first option and primary closer.
work_style: Added control and craft every year, took on more load each season, and grew methodically into the centerpiece.
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the traded-secondary-piece-to-MVP-level arc; verify if leaning on a specific season.

name: Dirk Nowitzki
distinctive_detail: Played all 21 seasons for one franchise, came from Germany as an unknown project, and stayed loyal through lean years until he finally closed out a title.
work_style: Refined a singular signature skill, stayed loyal to one organization for two decades, and delivered the title himself.
comp_tone: Beloved, earned. For the person who stayed with one place through everything and finally delivered.

name: Shaun Livingston
distinctive_detail: Suffered one of the most gruesome knee injuries in sports history that nearly ended his career, then fought back over years to become a steady, valuable role guard and three-time champion.
work_style: Rebuilt his entire game around a mid-range, ball-control style after the injury and became a dependable rotation piece.
2k_label: The One Who Came All The Way Back
comp_tone: Resilient. For the person whose career nearly ended and who rebuilt it into something steady and real.

name: Tony Parker
distinctive_detail: A late first-round international pick who spent nearly his entire career with one franchise, growing from a doubted rookie into a Finals MVP and the offensive engine.
work_style: Attacked the paint relentlessly, took big shots, and grew within one organization from project to Finals MVP.
comp_tone: Earned. For the person who grew up inside one place from doubted rookie to the engine that wins it all.
